header.title: PDF to Word — Convert PDF to Editable DOCX Online Free
header.description: Free online PDF to Word converter to transform PDF files into editable DOCX documents. Extract text with layout preservation for easy editing in Microsoft Word or Google Docs — 100% client-side, your files never leave your browser.
header.keywords: pdf to word, convert pdf to word, pdf to docx, pdf to editable word, pdf to word converter, extract text from pdf, pdf to doc, free pdf to word

notice.title: Text-based PDFs only.
notice.description: This tool extracts selectable text from PDF files using PDF.js. Scanned/image-based PDFs require OCR (not available in-browser). Complex layouts (multi-column, tables) may not preserve perfectly.

privacy.title: 100% client-side.
privacy.description: Your PDF never leaves your browser. Conversion is done entirely in JavaScript.

options.title: Output Options
options.addPageBreaksSub: Inserts a DOCX page break after each PDF page
options.addPageNumbersSub: Adds 'Page N' heading before each page
options.cleanWhitespaceSub: Merge multiple blank lines into one

preview.title: Text Preview
preview.noText: No text extracted from this page (may be an image-only page)

howToUse.title: How to Use PDF to Word
howToUse.step1Desc: Drop or upload a text-based PDF file you want to convert to DOCX.
howToUse.step2Desc: Choose typography settings: font family, font size, line breaks, page breaks, and page headings.
howToUse.step3Desc: Review the extracted text preview per page to check quality before downloading.
howToUse.step4Desc: Click 'Convert to Word' and download your editable .docx file — 100% client-side.

items.question: What types of PDFs work best?
items.answer: Text-based PDFs with selectable text produce the best results. Scanned or image-based PDFs require OCR which is not available in-browser.

items.question: Does the tool preserve formatting?
items.answer: Basic formatting like font, font size, and line breaks are preserved. Complex layouts such as multi-column text and tables may not convert perfectly.

items.question: What is the maximum PDF size?
items.answer: Most PDFs under 50 MB work well. Larger files take longer to process since text extraction and DOCX generation happen entirely in the browser.

items.question: Can I convert password-protected PDFs?
items.answer: No. You must remove the password first using the Unlock PDF tool before converting to Word.

items.question: What font options are available?
items.answer: You can choose between Sans-serif, Serif, and Mono font families, and adjust the font size. The selected font is embedded in the DOCX output.

items.question: Can I add page breaks between PDF pages?
items.answer: Yes. The 'Page breaks between pages' option inserts a DOCX page break after each PDF page for a more accurate page-by-page layout.

items.question: Is my PDF uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs client-side using PDF.js for text extraction and a DOCX builder library. Your file never leaves your browser.
